transcript:
Thank you for making out here today. So I'm gonna talk a little bit about what we're building at GameShift. Um, so just a little bit of background, uh, our team's been building here since 2021 on Solana, and this year we had the opportunity to acquire GameShift from Solana Labs.

With GameShift, uh, the first product that we're going to be launching here soon is our AI game engine called Omakase. Uh, we're really excited about this and it's gonna be able to create experiences like you've never been able to do before in gaming. Um, you can think of it as an engine that lets you take uh a small quantity of assets, then expand it out using state-of-the-art uh models, uh and then we curate those assets and we actually assemble it on runtime. So some of the benefits you get now is uh before in generative AI games, you have a lot of latency with like two to five seconds. Now we're able to do it in literally milliseconds. So it's extremely fast. The other is uh it's extremely cheap. Um, now we don't have to worry about uh exponential costs due to like prompting going up as you get like deeper into the game itself. Um, and then on top of that, it's very efficient. So because we uh uh we created a new technique called Constructive AI, and this allows us to be much more efficient with how the game is assembled. Uh and we'll be actually releasing this paper here soon.

Uh so alongside Omakase Game Engine, we're also going to be launching Omakase IP Marketplace. Uh and this now allows us to do attribution uh just due to the way Constructive AI works. Uh with this, we're going to be creating an IP marketplace that'll be able to track all the attribution so artists can create uh derivative work and uh you can remix different games across different game genres.

Um, alongside that we'll also have the public facing GameShift Pulse, which is an end-to-end uh uh analytics platform as well as a game discovery platform, storefront, and launcher. Uh and then finally, of course, we'll be supporting all of our Web3 uh API infrastructure that was created and built at Solana Labs.

Um, so January, this year we're gonna be opening up our seed round with Seedplex. We're very excited, so keep an eye on out for that. Thank you.

sources.title: Metaplex
sources.snippet:
[Back to Blog](https://metaplex.com/blog)

## October Highlights

- **Genesis**\- Genesis powered two presales in October, accounting for 18% of the total monthly revenue, the highest share since inception.

- **Protocol Revenue** \- Protocol revenue reached $2.0M in October.

- **$MPLX Buyback**\- $1.1M of Metaplex protocol fees were used to buy back 5.3M $MPLX (0.5% of total supply / 0.9% of outstanding supply) for the Metaplex DAO.


## Pump.Fun Update (November)

On November 7th, Pump.Fun [disclosed in its developer documentation](https://github.com/pump-fun/pump-public-docs/blob/main/README.md) that it would begin minting tokens using its new Mayhem Mode through SPL Token-22 rather than Metaplex’s Token Metadata. This change went into effect on November 12th and since then the percentage of total tokens launched daily by Pump.Fun that use Metaplex’s Token Metadata has declined to less than 15%.

Metaplex Foundation expects this to result in a decline in Metaplex protocol revenue near term with Pump.Fun contributing 44%, 60%, and 45% of total revenue during August, September, and October, respectively, after peaking at 86% in January 2025.

In light of the slowdown in memecoin activity, over the last few months the Metaplex Foundation has intentionally supported the expansion of the protocol from being purely an asset standard to a broader platform for Internet Capital Markets on Solana. Genesis, Metaplex’s protocol for fair onchain token launches, has found product market fit with over $1.1M in revenue generated since its launch in July 2025 and is now scaling. Metaplex is well positioned to become the canonical end-to-end platform for launching, discovering, and trading new assets on Solana.

The Metaplex Foundation remains well funded with more than three years of operating runway to support the Metaplex ecosystem even accounting for the loss of Pump.Fun associated revenue. This excludes the value of Foundation's and the Metaplex DAO's MPLX positions, which collectively account for more than 40% of the total supply. While this change will have a significant impact on revenue, the concentration risk was well understood, requiring no significant changes in the Metaplex Foundation’s operating plans or capital allocation.

Please note that the October revenue figures that follow in the rest of the newsletter below include revenue from Pump.Fun given the change occurred in November.

## October Protocol Activity

In October, Metaplex generated $2.0M in protocol revenue including $1.6M from Token Metadata (78%), $370K from Genesis (18%), and $72K from Core (4%). Genesis accounted for 18% of total revenue, its highest share since inception.

Combined, token creation (TM tokens) and token launches (Genesis) accounted for 91% of revenue while 9% was driven by the creation of NFTs (TM NFTs, Core, BG v2).

In October, 766K Token Metadata assets were created, including 720K fungible tokens (94%).

Core adoption increased in October, with 238K mints driving the cumulative mint total over 4M.

In October, creators and developers minted over 4M digital assets through Metaplex, including 3.0M compressed NFTs. This activity raised the protocol’s cumulative total to over 960M assets minted to date.

Unique user activity continued in October, with approximately 420K unique wallets authorizing transactions powered by Metaplex.Approximately 290K new wallets collected a Token Metadata asset and over 570K new wallets collected a Bubblegum NFT in the month of October.

## Developer Platform

**Genesis**

October marked another strong month for Genesis, powering multiple token launches and expanding its role across gaming, DeFi, and consumer apps.

[The month began with PlaySolana ($PLAY)](https://x.com/metaplex/status/1977434437748150707), a consumer gaming company building portable hardware devices and an onchain gaming ecosystem. [Their fixed-price presale sold out within minutes, selling over $2.6M (13.2K SOL) to more than 1,600 participant

sources.title: Prepare for Sailing - Launching November 19th!
sources.snippet:
## Prepare for Sailing - Launching November 19th!

04 November 2025

Sailing, Old School RuneScape�s first **NEW** skill, is launching on **November 19th!**

Before jumping into the content, we'd like to give you a run-down of the basic training methods, including what you might need to have prepared for launch day!

_If you can't see the video above, click [here](https://www.youtube.com/embed/wW_Y2OZGqE0?si=p2B1ONdsx1v92PlQ) to watch it!_

# Sailing Technical Pre-Release Information

We're committed to ensuring the launch of Sailing runs as smoothly as possible. To achieve this, we're adjusting our usual release process to allow extra time for testing and stability improvements before everyone sets sail.

Sailing will be added to the game two weeks before it becomes available to players. During this period, we'll be monitoring the content in a live, staff-only beta world to help us identify and resolve any potential issues ahead of launch.

Here's how launch day should look like:

Launch Day Plan (November 19th) - All times in UTC.

- **7:15am UTC** � System update timer goes live.
- **8:00am UTC** � Game worlds go offline briefly for the update.
- **8:00�8:15am UTC** � Worlds come back online, players can log in as normal, but won�t be able to start Sailing yet.
- **8:15am�10:00am UTC** � We'll be running some final checks on the live worlds. Players can continue playing on them, but Sailing will remain locked until our checks are complete.
- **~10:00am UTC** � Depending on those checks, we'll unlock Sailing for all members without rebooting the worlds, so you'll be able to start your new adventure directly!

Please note that once game worlds come back online, the update will have been deployed, despite not being accessible to players yet. As a result of this, the max cape and its perks will no longer be usable, and everyone will officially have Level 1 Sailing, which will be reflected on the HiScores right away (upon logout). You won't be able to start the Pandemonium quest that unlocks the skill until we make Sailing accessible, hopefully around 10.00am UTC.

We'll keep you updated via our usual channels (Game Update post or [Game Status Page](https://secure.runescape.com/m=news/game-status-information-centre?oldschool=1)) if we need extra time for our final checks.

# Sailors Wanted!

_If you want a higher resolution look at the image above, [click here!](https://cdn.runescape.com/assets/img/external/oldschool/2025/newsposts/2025-11-04/1.png)_

_If you want a higher resolution look at the image above, [click here!](https://cdn.runescape.com/assets/img/external/oldschool/2025/newsposts/2025-11-04/2.png)_

_If you want a higher resolution look at the image above, [click here!](https://cdn.runescape.com/assets/img/external/oldschool/2025/newsposts/2025-11-04/3.png)_

# The Basics

To get access to Sailing, you will need to complete a quest called Pandemonium. There are no requirements to start it, but please note the quest and skill are members'-only. On launch, simply head to Port Sarim and speak to Will and Anne nearby the docks.

We�ve specifically designed this quest to feel like an easy way of learning the mechanics of boat navigation without it feeling too overwhelming. It will teach you how to sail a Raft, which is the �starter boat� for Sailing, unlocked at Level 1. We know that many players will want to hit their spacebar as fast as possible to get the quest finished quickly. However, we'd recommend taking some time with this quest to familiarize yourself with navigation, especially the Sailing Side Panel, which is a key part of how you will engage with the skill.

Once you complete Pandemonium, you�ll get your very own Raft and Captain�s Log. Congratulations!

### The Pandemonium

During the Pandemonium quest, you will be introduced to a new location, also called the Pandemonium.

The Pandemonium acts as somewhat of a hub for Sailing. Not only does it have all the Sailing amenities you'll need, but it also has a ra

tags.impact_reason: GameShift's AI game engine and IP marketplace are interesting developments, but their impact remains to be seen until launch and adoption; the acquisition from Solana Labs adds some weight.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a crystalline 'Genesis Engine' as the anchor to represent the 'Omakase' and 'Constructive AI' concepts. It visualizes a central, powerful core taking a few base elements and rapidly constructing a complex game world. I included the diegetic text 'RUNTIME' as a holographic system status on the engine's surface to highlight the key technical innovation of real-time, in-game generation mentioned in the transcript.
visual_idea.description: A sophisticated, vertical 3:4 shot. A complex, geometric crystalline core—the Omakase AI engine—floats in the center of a dark, atmospheric void. Beams of cyan and magenta light emanate from it, rapidly assembling a fantastical game world of floating islands and abstract architecture. On a polished, obsidian facet of the core, the word 'RUNTIME' glows as a clean, holographic system status indicator. The background shows the vast, partially-formed game world fading into a digital nebula with a shallow depth of field.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A sophisticated, vertical 3:4 shot capturing a monumental, epic scale. In the central foreground, a complex, geometric crystalline core, serving as the Omakase AI engine, floats majestically within a vast, dark, and atmospheric void. Its intricate, light-refracting facets shimmer with internal energy. Potent, volumetric neon beams of cyan and magenta light powerfully emanate from the core, dynamically assembling a fantastical game world. This nascent world consists of impossibly detailed floating islands and abstract architecture, rapidly materializing in the mid-ground. On a perfectly polished, highly reflective obsidian facet of the core, the word 'RUNTIME' glows intensely as a clean, holographic system status indicator, casting subtle light onto the surrounding surfaces. The background reveals the immense, partially-formed game world, fading into a swirling, digital nebula with a pronounced shallow depth of field, emphasizing the core's dominance. The composition emphasizes dramatic height and ample negative space at the top, making the engine feel colossal. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, realistic materials, cinematic contrast, volumetric lighting, highly reflective surfaces, intricate textures.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a crystalline 'Genesis Engine' as the anchor to represent the 'Omakase' and 'Constructive AI' concepts. It visualizes a central, powerful core taking a few base elements and rapidly constructing a complex game world. I included the diegetic text 'RUNTIME' as a holographic system status on the engine's surface to highlight the key technical innovation of real-time, in-game generation mentioned in the transcript.
visual_idea.description: A 1:1 macro shot focusing on the intricate surface of the Omakase AI engine. We see the complex, fractal-like crystalline geometry in extreme detail. A single, focused beam of cyan light emits from a point on the surface, visibly carrying micro-scale data particles that are the fundamental building blocks of the game assets. The mood is precise and technical.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A 1:1 macro shot, tightly framed and centrally focused, showcasing the intricate surface of the Omakase AI engine, filling the entire frame with texture. The surface reveals complex, fractal-like crystalline geometry in extreme detail, possessing the sharp facets and translucent depths of polished obsidian, reflecting light with high-gloss intensity. A single, intensely focused beam of vibrant cyan volumetric neon light dramatically emits from a specific point on the surface, cutting through atmospheric mist and visibly carrying micro-scale data particles – the fundamental building blocks of game assets – along its illuminated path. The overall mood is precise, technical, and cutting-edge. Rendered with the fidelity of the Genesis Engine, emphasizing symmetry, deep shadows, and cinematic contrast. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, subsurface scattering, high-gloss reflection, fractal geometry, volumetric lighting.

editorial.headline: GameShift Unveils ‘Omakase’ AI Engine and Pilot Seedplex Venture Token Raise
editorial.standfirst: The former Solana Labs unit pivots to runtime generative gaming with a new "Constructive AI" model. Plus, the protocol confirms it will be a launch partner for the Seedplex equity-token hybrid platform.
key_takeaways: GameShift, acquired by Honeycomb Protocol in Sept 2025, is launching the "Omakase" AI Game Engine.
New "Constructive AI" technique reduces generative asset latency from seconds to milliseconds.
GameShift will open its seed round in Jan 2026 as a flagship issuer on Seedplex, the new venture token platform.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
In a significant pivot from its origins as a pure infrastructure API, **GameShift**—recently acquired by **Honeycomb Protocol** from **Solana Labs**—has unveiled a proprietary AI game engine designed to solve the latency crisis in generative gaming. Dubbed **Omakase**, the engine utilizes a novel technique the team calls "Constructive AI," which reportedly slashes asset generation time from the industry standard of 2-5 seconds down to mere milliseconds. 

This technical leap allows for runtime assembly of game assets, moving beyond pre-generated static environments to truly dynamic experiences. By curating small quantities of base assets and expanding them via state-of-the-art models in real-time, GameShift aims to eliminate the exponential compute costs associated with deep-game prompting. The accompanying **Omakase IP Marketplace** addresses the attribution dilemma in generative AI, enabling a remix culture where original artists retain verifiable on-chain attribution for derivative works created by the engine.

Perhaps most notable for institutional investors is GameShift’s capital formation strategy. The team announced they will open their seed round in January 2026 via **Seedplex**, a new platform for "Venture Tokens." As one of the first issuers on Seedplex, GameShift is testing a hybrid model that bridges traditional equity rights with token liquidity—a mechanism that could set a precedent for how infrastructure projects capitalize in the post-2025 market cycle.
